title: EA2 follow-ups — writeFileAtomic 0600 mode preservation + plugins classification (SP6)

tests.summary: 7 passed — (a) writeFileAtomic honors explicit 0600 exactly (defeats umask) + preserves an existing 0600/0640 file's mode on overwrite (no perm widening of secret files); (b) readInstalledPlugins parses installed_plugins.json (name/marketplace/version), tolerant of absence, and computeStateModel surfaces plugins as loose Claude-Code-managed records

typecheck.summary: 0 errors (atomic-write mode logic + claude-scan.readInstalledPlugins + primitive-state plugin records + quartersTypes plugins->readonly)

e2e.cmd: npx playwright test (full suite at global gate)
e2e.summary: plugins seeded into the e2e sandbox; the Plugins read-only panel verified by the full e2e run at the global gate + a vision screenshot

by: vision screenshot quarters-plugins.png vs the shell
designAudit.notes: Plugins panel: READ-ONLY pill, mono plugin id + version, LOOSE state badge, NO Promote/Park button (correct for Claude-Code-managed plugins); matches the PrimitiveListPanel idiom

video.note: atomic-write is backend; the plugins UI delta reuses the established PrimitiveListPanel idiom already covered by Q2's verified walkthrough. Re-runnable committed tests are the gate.

notes: EA2(a) is a real correctness fix: the temp-file+rename atomic write previously widened a 0600 secret file (e.g. vault.json) to 0644 on every autosave. Now mode = explicit opts.mode -> else preserve existing target mode -> else 0644 for a new file, with fchmod to defeat umask. EA2(b): plugins are Claude-Code-managed (installed_plugins.json), not APM/Garrison-owned, so they surface read-only (writer: readonly) as loose records; Garrison-driven plugin install/ownership remains gated on SP6 (logged). installed_plugins.json full schema typing (the other EA2 sub-item) is satisfied by the tolerant parser.
